title: Castles
subtitle: Stone fortresses of the medieval world
intro: Castles were fortified residences built for defense, control, and prestige.
content: Castles became important during the Middle Ages as centers of military and political power.
They were often built on hills or near rivers to improve defense.
Inside, they included living quarters, storage, chapels, and defensive towers.
facts: Many castles had moats and drawbridges.
The keep was usually the strongest part.
